title: Lock Driver Availability Toggle When Accepted Trip Exists

requirements.summary: Lock availability toggle when driver has accepted trips
acceptanceCriteria: When the driver has at least one accepted trip, the availability toggle is visually disabled (greyed out) and cannot be clicked.
The toggle displays Available (true) regardless of the driver's last saved value while any accepted trip exists.
Helper text 'Availability is locked while you have an accepted trip.' is visible beneath the toggle when locked.
Helper text is hidden and toggle is enabled when no accepted trips exist.
No changes are made to the Available Trips section, My Accepted Trips section layout, or any customer/admin UI.

file_operations.description: Add logic to compute `hasAcceptedTrip` by checking if any trip in the driver's My Accepted Trips list has status 'accepted'. When true: force the displayed availability to true (Available), disable the availability toggle, and show helper text 'Availability is locked while you have an accepted trip.' beneath the toggle. When false: enable the toggle and hide the helper text. Fetch the driver's accepted trips using `useGetMyTrips` hook and filter for trips with `status === 'accepted'`.

Accept a new prop `hasAcceptedTrip: boolean`. When true: disable the availability toggle field (set disabled attribute), force its value to true (Available), and display helper text 'Availability is locked while you have an accepted trip.' beneath the toggle. When false: enable the toggle and hide the helper text. Ensure the form does not allow submitting availability = false when `hasAcceptedTrip` is true.

Pass the `hasAcceptedTrip` boolean prop from the parent DriverProfileSection to EditDriverProfileForm so the availability toggle is locked in both the profile card and edit modal when accepted trips exist.

requirements.summary: Re-enable availability toggle after completing all accepted trips
acceptanceCriteria: After marking a trip as completed, if no other accepted trips remain, the availability toggle becomes interactive again.
The toggle reflects the driver's last saved availability value (from their profile) after unlocking.
If the driver had multiple accepted trips and only one is completed, the toggle remains locked until all accepted trips are resolved.
No changes are made to the Mark as Completed mutation logic or the DriverTripList layout.

file_operations.description: Ensure the `hasAcceptedTrip` computation reactively updates when trips change. After `useCompleteTrip` mutation succeeds, React Query will invalidate and refetch the driver's trips. The `hasAcceptedTrip` boolean will automatically recalculate: if no accepted trips remain, the toggle unlocks and displays the driver's persisted availability value from their profile. No additional logic needed beyond existing query invalidation.

acceptanceCriteria: A post-build checklist with the two steps is either rendered as a visible UI note on the Driver Dashboard (e.g., a small dev/QA callout) or included as a clearly labeled comment in the relevant component file.
The checklist does not affect production UI for end users if rendered only as a code comment.

file_operations.description: Add a clearly labeled comment block at the top of the component file with the post-build checklist: '(1) Accept trip → toggle is disabled and availability shows Available; (2) Complete trip → toggle is re-enabled.' Format as a JSDoc-style comment or inline comment for developer visibility during code review.
